slides.title: Meet Python — Your New Superpower
slides.body: Python is a programming language that lets you give instructions to a computer. What makes it special? **It reads almost like plain English** — no cryptic symbols required.
slides.tip: Python is named after Monty Python's Flying Circus, not the snake!

slides.title: Code That Speaks For Itself
slides.body: Python was designed so humans — not just computers — can read the code. No semicolons, no curly braces, just clean indented lines that show structure at a glance.
slides.code:
name = "Alice"
age = 30

if age >= 18:
    print(name + " is an adult.")
slides.tip: Even first-timers can guess what this code does — that is the whole point.

slides.title: Run It and See — Instantly
slides.body: Python is **interpreted**, meaning it runs your code line by line right away. Write a line, run it, see the result — no waiting, no compile step.
slides.tip: Python is case-sensitive: `print` works, but `Print` or `PRINT` will error.

slides.title: Python Powers the Real World
slides.body: Instagram, YouTube, NASA — they all use Python. It handles **web apps, AI, data science, automation, and games**. The language you learn here scales all the way up.
slides.tip: A few lines of Python can automate tasks that would take hours by hand.

slides.title: You Picked the Right Language
slides.body: Python is the **world's most popular first language** because it rewards you fast. Clean syntax, instant feedback, and a massive community mean you are never stuck for long.
slides.tip: Every expert was once a beginner who typed `print("Hello, World!")` for the first time.
